    Mobile search

      • Home
      • About the Foundation
        • Share on Facebook
        • Post to X (formerly Twitter)
        • Share on LinkedIn
        • Send as Email

      About the Foundation

      There is no health without mental health. The APA Foundation is working in your community to provide the support and resources we all need to prioritize mental wellbeing where you live, learn, work, worship, and play.

      Backed by the medical expertise of the APA and industry-leading strategic partners, we administer community-changing programs, public education campaigns, grants, and awards aimed at increasing mental health literacy, connecting individuals to needed care, and shifting attitudes toward mental health.

      Our Mission

      Promote the mental health and well-being of individuals and communities.

      Our Vision

      A mentally healthy nation for all – where we live, learn, work, worship, and play.

      Our Impact

      • 2,000+
        Early career psychiatrists have been trained by the APA Foundation's Fellowship program since its inception in 1968.
      • 150K
        Employees have been impacted in the Notice. Talk. Act.® at Work program in the last year.
      • 99%
        Reduction in major mental health events recorded by schools trained in Notice. Talk. Act.® at School.

      Our Programs

      • Schools

        Notice. Talk. Act.® at School supports youth mental health by training school staff to notice early warning signs, talk about mental health, and act by connecting students to resources.

      • Justice

        Our Justice programs build bridges between judges and psychiatrists to address the overrepresentation of individuals with mental illness in the justice system.

      • Workplace

        The Center for Workplace Mental Health provides employers with the training, tools, resources, and information needed to promote and support the mental health of employees and their families.

      • Faith

        The APAF Mental Health and Faith Community Partnership brings faith leaders and mental health professionals together to support wellness inside and outside the sanctuary.

      • Play

        Pressure to perform at the highest level can negatively affect the mental health of athletes and entertainers. Our Play initiative connects young performers and adults to resources they need.

      • Fellowships

        Through experiential learning, mentorship, and professional development, the APA Foundation Resident Fellowship Program produces psychiatrists uniquely positioned to be leaders in the field.

      Learn More

      • Partner with Us

        Collaborating with the APA Foundation brings your organization's expertise and influence together with our mission to exponentially increase collective impact. Learn how we work with our partners to create a mentally healthy nation.

      • Impact Report

        In 2023, APAF focused efforts on launching a first-of-its-kind national public awareness campaign, expanded Notice. Talk. Act.® at School to full no-cost, national availability, increased our fellowship program size, and so much more. Learn about the APA Foundation’s full 2023 Impact.

      • Board of Directors

        Administration

      Mental Health Care Works

      Learn about APAF’s first-of-its-kind national public awareness campaign, focused on empowering those with mental health concerns to get help.
